campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they discussed staffing vacancies and the city's efforts to recruit and retain employees. The council also addressed housing security concerns and promoted the upcoming Boogie Festival happening that weekend.
keyTopics: Staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention efforts
Housing security and affordability
Boogie Festival this weekend

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council approved preliminary engineering reports and set up a public hearing process for an upcoming ordinance vote on May 20th. The council also discussed whether Saratoga should become a charter city and reviewed potential tax options and revenue estimates.
keyTopics: Preliminary approval of Engineer's Report for fiscal year
Public hearing scheduled for ordinance adoption
Potential charter city status under consideration
Tax options and revenue estimates being evaluated

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 16, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on June 16, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from earlier in June and authorize the Town Manager to sign several contract amendments and new agreements. The main business involved updating legal service contracts, approving a new software services agreement, and adopting a resolution on an unspecified corrected matter.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes (June 2 and June 8)
Multiple contract amendments for legal services
New five-year software services agreement
Litigation legal services authorization

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 23, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met on June 23, 2026, to discuss several items including designating a historic property at 647 South Sixth Street as a city landmark, potentially changing how the city fills vacant mayor and council seats using ranked choice voting, awarding an advertising contract at the airport, and approving a long-term power agreement with Shell Energy North America.
keyTopics: Historic landmark designation and tax incentive contract for 647 South Sixth Street property
Proposed charter amendment to allow ranked choice voting for filling mayor and councilmember vacancies
Large format advertising sign contract at San José Mineta International Airport
Long-term power purchase agreement with Shell Energy North America

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: Mountain View's City Council met to handle budget decisions, celebrate community milestones, and address legal matters. Key decisions included adopting a water management plan, approving the capital improvement program for next year, and moving forward with infrastructure projects around the city.
keyTopics: Two pending lawsuits facing the city (details discussed in closed session)
Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget and appropriations limit
5-year water management and drought contingency plan approved
Capital improvement projects including Public Safety Building, golf course parking lot repairs, and salt pond restoration
Recognition of City Attorney Jennifer Logue's appointment to the judiciary

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 23, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-session meeting on June 23, 2026, followed by a regular council meeting at 7 P.M. The closed session portion was not open to the public, so the specific topics discussed there are not disclosed in this agenda.
keyTopics: Special closed-session meeting held
Regular council meeting convened at 7 P.M.
Flag salute and public meeting protocols observed

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — July 7, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a study session to review and update the city's Health and Safety Element—a key planning document that guides development and safety priorities through 2040. The council also recognized the Parks and Recreation Department for their work across programs like youth recreation, senior wellness, and sports, as well as honored Monta Vista High School's Science Olympiad Team for their achievement.
keyTopics: Updating the Health and Safety Element of the General Plan (2015-2040)
Recognition of Parks and Recreation Department divisions and programs
Monta Vista High School Science Olympiad Team achievement

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 30, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on June 30, 2026, using both in-person and remote participation options. Residents could attend via Zoom or phone, and submit written comments online before the meeting.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Remote participation options via Zoom and phone
Written public comments accepted through eComments system

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to update the city's five-year housing and community development plan, review requests from council members for new work items, and consider changes to how the city fills council vacancies. The council also approved meeting minutes and previewed upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Update to 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan (housing and federal funding priorities)
Changes to Council Vacancy Ordinance
New workplan requests from individual councilmembers
City Council meeting calendar and schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — June 10, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to handle routine administrative items and consider a zoning change at 3300 El Camino Real. The main decision involved updating building setback requirements (the distance buildings must be set back from the property line) along Hansen Way from their current amount to 20 feet.
keyTopics: Building setback requirement change at 3300 El Camino Real/Hansen Way to 20 feet
Approval of Planning & Transportation Commission meeting minutes from April 29, 2026
City Council schedule and staff assignments
Zoning change classified as exempt from lengthy environmental review
